title: Good: chat_get called even on empty query result to get state string
description: In load_chats(), chat_get is always called even when query.ids is empty. The comment explains why: without calling get we don't receive the server state string needed for Chat/changes (delta sync). If get were skipped on empty results, chat_state would remain None and every SSE StateChange would fall back to a full reload. The non-obvious insight is that get (not query) is the source of the canonical state string per the JMAP spec, and an empty result set is still a valid reason to call it.
